content:
3 min read
Just now

--

Most workflow tools feel heavy, complex, or locked behind drag‑and‑drop UIs. Evixor takes a different approach. It’s a lightweight, code‑native execution runtime for building traceable, stateful pipelines — designed for developers who prefer TypeScript over visual editors.
If you want to explore Evixor immediately, here are the key entry points:

• Evixor Homepage → https://evixor.org/

• Getting Started Guide → https://evixor.org/static/#/documents/getting-started

• Workflow Demo (GitHub) → https://github.com/evixor-dev/workflow-demo

• Evixor Runtime on npm → https://www.npmjs.com/package/@evixor/evixor-runtime
This quick start will show you how to build and run a fully traceable workflow in under ten minutes — no visual builders, no YAML, just clean TypeScript pipelines powered by Evixor.
Evixor takes a different approach. It’s a lightweight execution runtime for building traceable, stateful, code‑native pipelines. No visual editors. No YAML. Just TypeScript.
This quick start shows how to build and run a fully traceable workflow in under ten minutes.
🚀 What You’ll Build
You’ll create a simple arithmetic workflow:

• Add two numbers

• Multiply the result

• Subtract

• Divide

• Run a Fibonacci sub‑pipeline

• Inspect the full execution timeline as a Gantt chart
All powered by Evixor’s runtime.
📦 Clone the Demo
git clone https://github.com/evixor-dev/workflow-demo.git
cd workflow-demoYou can also browse the source here: workflow-demo
🧠 What This Demo Shows
Evixor’s runtime introduces several core concepts:

• Pipeline definition — chain .next() and .step() to describe workflow logic

• Step functions — reusable units of work

• State management — pass data through ctx.state, ctx.current.input, ctx.current.drop

• Sub‑pipelines — call one pipeline from another

• Control re‑runs — loop until a condition is met

• Host loop — drive execution to completion

• Timeline inspection — view the full execution history
🛠 Prerequisites

• Node.js ≥ 18

• pnpm（npm or yarn also work）
▶️ Run the Demo
pnpm install
pnpm startThis will:

• Start a math workflow with { a: 3, b: 7 }

• Execute: add → multiply → subtract → divide → fibonacci

• Print the full execution timeline as JSON
📁 Project Structure
workflow-demo/
├── src/
│ ├── index.ts # Entry point
│ ├── register.ts # Pipeline registry
│ ├── pipelines/
│ │ ├── math.ts
│ │ └── fibonacci.ts
│ └── steps/
│ ├── add.ts
│ ├── subtract.ts
│ ├── multiply.ts
│ └── divide.ts🧩 Step Functions
Each step is a simple function:
export function add(runtime: EvixorRuntimeApi) {
const { a, b } = runtime.getInput<{ a: number; b: number }>();
runtime.submitDrop(a + b);
}🧱 Pipelines
A pipeline chains transformations and steps:
export default (pl: EvixorPipeline) =>
pl
.next((ctx) => { /* prepare input */ })
.step("add", add)
.next((ctx) => { /* feed result */ })
.step("multiply", multiply)
.step("submit", (runtime) => runtime.submitDrop(runtime.getInput()));🔗 Registry
Pipelines are registered by name:
const pipelineEntries = [
["math", mathBuilder],
["fibonacci", fibonacciBuilder],
];
export default new Map(pipelineEntries);🏁 Entry Point
const persistLayer = new InMemoryPersistLayer();
const hostLayer = new InMemoryHostLayer(persistLayer, pipelineRegistry);const workflowId = await startWorkflow(undefined, "math", { a: 3, b: 7 });
await hostLayer.doLoop({});
const timelines = await persistLayer.listTimeline(workflowId);
console.log(JSON.stringify(timelines));
📊 Visualize the Timeline on evixor.org
Evixor includes a built‑in timeline viewer.

• Copy the JSON printed in your console

• Open https://evixor.org

• Sign in with GitHub

• Create a tenant

• Go to Timelines → Load JSON

• Paste the JSON
You’ll see a full Gantt chart of your workflow:

• Each pipeline is a row

• Each step is a colored bar

• Click any bar to inspect input/output/duration
🌐 Run Your Workflow Directly on evixor.org
You can also upload your pipelines and run them in the browser.

• Zip:
pipelines/
steps/
register.ts
• Upload to Bundles

• Activate

• Run any pipeline with custom input

• Inspect the timeline
Evixor handles execution, persistence, and visualization automatically.
🎯 Final Thoughts
Evixor gives you:

• Code‑native workflow definition

• Full execution traceability

• Sub‑pipelines

• Looping

• State management

• Timeline visualization

• Zero UI drag‑and‑drop
It’s a workflow engine designed for developers — not for clicking boxes on a canvas.
If you want to explore more:

• Evixor Pipeline Concepts

• Evixor Runtime Architecture

• Evixor Playground
